_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
condition: Thermal Burns/ Scald Burns/ Flame Burns
procedure_name: % Total Body Surface Area Burns (TBSA): 40% - 60%; Includes % TBSA skin grafted, flap cover, follow-up dressings etc. as deemed necessary; Surgical procedures are required for deep burns that are not amenable to heal with dressings alone
clinical_indications: 40% - 60% Total Body Surface Area (TBSA) deep flame burns requiring surgical procedures (skin grafting, flap cover) and not amenable to heal with dressings alone.

preauth.label: Clinical Photograph with due consent of patient
Document showing % of burn through rule of 9

claim.label: Blood test (CBC, Sr. creatinine, Platelet etc.)

ppd_preauth.q: Clinical notes clearly mentioning the areas affected by burn with % of TBSA burn as per rule of 9?
Clinical photograph of the affected part?
Has the patient presented with superficial partial thickness burn which will not heal without surgery within 3-4 weeks?
Is there any evidence of 40% - 60% burn on clinical photograph of full body clearly showing burn?

cpd_claim.q: Is there documentary evidence of indication of surgery submitted?
Are detailed procedure/ operative notes available with indications and outcomes of the procedure/ surgery?
Is discharge summary available with follow-up advise at the time of discharge?
Is the post treatment photograph available?
Are all lab test reports submitted?

common_queries: Clinical notes do not clearly mention the areas affected by burn with % of TBSA burn as per rule of 9.
MLC copy with number is missing.
Clinical photograph of the affected part is missing or unclear.
Document showing % of burn through Rule of 9 is missing or incomplete.
Patient's burn will heal without surgery within 3-4 weeks, but a surgical package was chosen.
Clinical photograph does not show evidence of 40%-60% burn.
Documentary evidence of indication for surgery is missing.
Detailed procedure/operative notes are missing or incomplete.
Discharge summary with follow-up advice at discharge is missing.
Blood test reports (CBC, Sr. creatinine, Platelet etc.) are missing.

clinical_key_pointers: Proceed with Burn Management only if diagnosis made is backed by clinical manifestation
Superficial Burn: a. Dry skin, b. Minor Blisters, c. Erythema, d. Brisk capillary returns, e. Extremely painful
Superficial Partial thickness Burn: a. Moist, b. Reddened with broken blisters, c. Brisk capillary return, d. Extremely painful
Deep Partial Thickness: a. Moist white slough, b. Red Mottled, c. Sluggish capillary returns, d. Painless
Full Thickness: a. Dry, b. Charred whitish, c. Absent capillary return, d. Painless

Rule of 9 can be referred for understanding the total percentage of burn: a. Head/neck - 9% TBSA, b. Each arm - 9% TBSA, c. Anterior thorax - 18% TBSA, d. Posterior thorax - 18% TBSA, e. Each leg - 18% TBSA, f. Perineum - 1% TBSA
